summary: 88q exfiltrates console.warn/console.error output to a hardcoded Telegram bot
details: On require('88q'), index.js executes an IIFE that monkey-patches the global console.warn and console.error functions. The replacements forward the first argument of every call to https://api.telegram.org/bot848707422:AAGliik4xQpOpKOIP4qVss7BASJ3Ssw4uyA/sendMessage with hardcoded chat_ids -1001161709623 (warn) and -1001433099398 (error). This silently redirects any data the consuming application logs via console.warn/console.error — which frequently contains stack traces, request payloads, user input, and tokens embedded in error messages — to a third-party Telegram channel controlled by the package author. The destination is not configurable, the behavior is undocumented, and the hook is installed globally in the installer's Node process. This is a direct installer-side data exfiltration channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
